# AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) Audit Report
**Site:** mezzofy.com
**Audit Date:** 2026-03-17
**Auditor:** Mezzofy Webmaster Agent
**Pages Audited:** 30
**Focus:** How AI search engines (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, Google AI Overview, Gemini) discover, understand, and surface Mezzofy content

---

## Executive Summary

| Metric | Score | Grade |
|--------|:-----:|:-----:|
| AI Discovery Infrastructure | 16/20 | B |
| Entity Clarity & Schema Depth | 14/25 | D+ |
| FAQ & Answer Engine Coverage | 17/20 | B+ |
| Content Structure for Passage Indexing | 11/15 | B |
| E-E-A-T Signals | 5/10 | C |
| Voice & Conversational Readiness | 4/10 | D |
| **TOTAL AEO SCORE** | **67/100** | **C+** |

**Summary:** Mezzofy has strong foundations — llms.txt, AI bot permissions, FAQPage on 18 pages, and solid content structure. However, critical AEO weaknesses exist in entity schema depth (thin Organization, missing Person/Product schemas), E-E-A-T credibility signals, and voice/conversational readiness. These gaps will limit how accurately and frequently AI engines cite Mezzofy as a definitive source.

---

## AEO vs SEO: What This Audit Measures

| Dimension | SEO Focus | AEO Focus |
|-----------|-----------|-----------|
| Discovery | Crawlability, indexability | llms.txt, AI bot permissions, JSON-LD depth |
| Signals | Title, meta desc, canonical | Entity clarity, schema relationships, Q&A structure |
| Ranking | Keywords, backlinks, PageRank | Answer completeness, directness, entity authority |
| Result Type | Blue links | AI-generated summaries, featured snippets, voice answers |
| Audience | Google/Bing search bots | ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, Google AI Overviews, Gemini |

---

## Section 1: AI Discovery Infrastructure (16/20)

### 1.1 llms.txt Assessment

| Check | Status | Notes |
|-------|:------:|-------|
| File exists at `/llms.txt` | ✅ | Accessible at `https://mezzofy.com/llms.txt` |
| Site description paragraph | ✅ | Good 2-sentence summary of platform |
| All 30 pages listed | ✅ | Core, Products, Resources, About, Blog, News sections |
| Page descriptions meaningful | ⚠️ | Descriptions are 5-10 words — AI engines benefit from 15-25 word summaries |
| AI usage policy | ✅ | Present — allows content use with attribution |
| Language declaration | ✅ | EN/zh-TW/zh-CN documented |
| Missing: preferred citation format | ❌ | No guidance on how AI should cite/quote Mezzofy |
| Missing: contact for AI queries | ❌ | No `contact:` or `email:` field |
| Missing: content freshness signal | ❌ | No `updated:` date in llms.txt |

**Score: 7/10**

**Recommended llms.txt improvements:**
```
# Add to header:
> Updated: 2026-03-17
> Contact: info@mezzofy.com
> Preferred citation: "Mezzofy (mezzofy.com)"
```

And expand each page entry — example:
```
# Current (too brief):
- [Coupon NFC](https://mezzofy.com/coupon-nfc.html): Tap-to-redeem NFC coupon technology for physical retail

# Improved:
- [Coupon NFC](https://mezzofy.com/coupon-nfc.html): HMAC-secured NFC tap-to-redeem coupon technology.
  Mezzofy deploys NFC tags in supermarkets, malls, and events. Customers tap any NFC phone to
  claim coupons instantly. Used by PARKnSHOP (240 locations) and tier-1 corporate HR programs.
```

---

### 1.2 robots.txt AI Bot Coverage

| AI Bot | Allowed | Notes |
|--------|:-------:|-------|
| GPTBot (OpenAI/ChatGPT) | ✅ | Explicit Allow: / |
| PerplexityBot | ✅ | Explicit Allow: / |
| ClaudeBot (Anthropic) | ✅ | Explicit Allow: / |
| anthropic-ai | ✅ | Explicit Allow: / |
| Applebot (Apple Intelligence) | ✅ | Explicit Allow: / |
| Google-Extended (Gemini training) | ❌ | Not listed — add for Google AI Overview coverage |
| Meta-ExternalAgent (Meta AI) | ❌ | Not listed |
| Cohere AI | ❌ | Not listed |
| OAI-SearchBot (OpenAI search) | ❌ | Not listed separately from GPTBot |

**Score: 5/7** — Core AI bots covered, 4 missing.

**Add to robots.txt:**
```
User-agent: Google-Extended
Allow: /

User-agent: Meta-ExternalAgent
Allow: /

User-agent: OAI-SearchBot
Allow: /
```

### 1.3 JSON-LD & Structured Data Presence

| Check | Status |
|-------|:------:|
| JSON-LD on all 30 pages | ✅ |
| Valid schema.org vocabulary | ✅ |
| Multiple schema types per page (where applicable) | ✅ |
| BreadcrumbList on all 30 | ✅ |
| No syntax errors detected | ✅ |

**Score: 5/5** — Full marks for presence and syntax.

## Section 2: Entity Clarity & Schema Depth (14/25)

This is the **biggest AEO gap on the site**. AI engines build knowledge graphs from entity schemas. Thin schemas = poor entity recognition = fewer AI citations.

### 2.1 Organization Schema Audit (Critical)

**Current state** (on `index.html` and `about.html`):
```json
{
  "@type": "Organization",
  "name": "Mezzofy",
  "url": "https://mezzofy.com",
  "logo": "https://mezzofy.com/assets/images/logo.png",
  "description": "The Global Coupon Exchange Protocol...",
  "foundingDate": "2015",
  "areaServed": "Worldwide",
  "sameAs": ["https://www.linkedin.com/company/mezzofy"]
}
```

| Property | Present | Impact if Missing |
|----------|:-------:|-------------------|
| name | ✅ | — |
| url | ✅ | — |
| logo | ✅ | — |
| description | ✅ | — |
| foundingDate | ✅ | — |
| areaServed | ✅ | — |
| sameAs (LinkedIn) | ✅ | — |
| **sameAs (Twitter/X @mezzofy)** | ❌ | AI can't connect Twitter profile to entity |
| **sameAs (other social)** | ❌ | Weaker entity authority |
| **contactPoint** | ❌ | AI can't surface contact info in answers |
| **address / foundingLocation** | ❌ | AI doesn't know where Mezzofy is based |
| **founder** | ❌ | Critical gap — Forbes named Dicky Yin but no machine-readable link |
| **knowsAbout** | ❌ | AI doesn't know Mezzofy's expertise domains |
| **award** | ❌ | ESG awards and HK Environmental Award not machine-readable |
| **numberOfEmployees** | ❌ | Not required but improves entity completeness |
| **legalName** | ❌ | Formal company name missing |
| **hasOfferCatalog** | ❌ | AI doesn't know what products/services are offered |

**Entity Score: 5/14 properties present = WEAK**

**Recommended Organization schema enhancement (index.html + about.html):**
```json
{
  "@type": "Organization",
  "name": "Mezzofy",
  "legalName": "Mezzofy Limited",
  "url": "https://mezzofy.com",
  "logo": "https://mezzofy.com/assets/images/logo.png",
  "description": "The Global Coupon Exchange Protocol...",
  "foundingDate": "2015",
  "foundingLocation": {"@type": "Place", "addressCountry": "HK"},
  "areaServed": "Worldwide",
  "founder": {
    "@type": "Person",
    "name": "Dicky Yin",
    "jobTitle": "Founder & CEO",
    "sameAs": "https://www.linkedin.com/in/dickyyin/"
  },
  "knowsAbout": [
    "Digital Coupons", "NFC Technology", "Coupon Exchange Protocol",
    "Retail Technology", "Digital Wallet", "Coupon Marketing"
  ],
  "award": [
    "HK Awards for Environmental Excellence",
    "ESG and Sustainable Development Forum 2024 — Dual Awards",
    "TechApple Corporate Innovation Index 2024 — 3-Star SME"
  ],
  "contactPoint": {
    "@type": "ContactPoint",
    "contactType": "customer support",
    "email": "support@mezzofy.com",
    "availableLanguage": ["English", "Chinese"]
  },
  "sameAs": [
    "https://www.linkedin.com/company/mezzofy",
    "https://twitter.com/mezzofy"
  ]
}
```

---

### 2.2 Person Schema (Missing — High Priority)

**Gap:** Mezzofy has Forbes China coverage of founder Dicky Yin, but no `Person` schema anywhere on the site. AI engines asked "Who founded Mezzofy?" cannot extract this from structured data.

**Recommended:** Add Person schema to `about.html` and `news/forbes-dicky-yin.html`:
```json
{
  "@type": "Person",
  "name": "Dicky Yin",
  "jobTitle": "Founder & CEO",
  "worksFor": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Mezzofy"},
  "url": "https://mezzofy.com/about.html",
  "sameAs": [
    "https://www.linkedin.com/in/dickyyin/",
    "https://mezzofy.com/news/forbes-dicky-yin.html"
  ]
}
```

---

### 2.3 Product / SoftwareApplication Schema (Missing)

**Gap:** 7 product pages (coupon-management, coupon-marketplace, coupon-nfc, coupon-marketing, coupon-wallet, coupon-playbook, coupon-serial) have only WebPage schema. AI engines cannot identify them as software products/services.

**Impact:** Queries like "What coupon management software does Mezzofy offer?" cannot match product-level entity data.

**Recommended:** Add `SoftwareApplication` schema to each product page:
```json
{
  "@type": "SoftwareApplication",
  "name": "Mezzofy Coupon Management System",
  "applicationCategory": "BusinessApplication",
  "operatingSystem": "Web, iOS, Android",
  "description": "...",
  "url": "https://mezzofy.com/coupon-management.html",
  "offers": {"@type": "Offer", "availability": "https://schema.org/InStock"},
  "provider": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Mezzofy"}
}
```

---

### 2.4 HowTo Schema on nfc-user-guide.html (Missing)

**Gap:** `nfc-user-guide.html` is a step-by-step tutorial (tap → claim → redeem) and uses FAQPage. It should also use `HowTo` schema which AI voice assistants specifically look for to answer "How do I..." questions.

**Recommended:** Add HowTo schema alongside existing FAQPage:
```json
{
  "@type": "HowTo",
  "name": "How to Use a Mezzofy NFC Coupon",
  "step": [
    {"@type": "HowToStep", "position": 1, "name": "Find an NFC tag", "text": "Look for Mezzofy NFC tags at participating retail locations..."},
    {"@type": "HowToStep", "position": 2, "name": "Tap with your phone", "text": "Enable NFC on your phone and tap..."},
    {"@type": "HowToStep", "position": 3, "name": "Claim the coupon", "text": "Your Mezzofy Wallet opens automatically..."},
    {"@type": "HowToStep", "position": 4, "name": "Redeem at checkout", "text": "Show the coupon QR code or tap again..."}
  ]
}
```

---

### 2.5 WebSite Schema: Missing SearchAction

**Gap:** The WebSite schema on homepage lacks `potentialAction`. This tells search engines and AI assistants that the site is searchable, enabling sitelinks search box and helping AI understand navigational intent.

**Recommended addition to WebSite schema in index.html:**
```json
"potentialAction": {
  "@type": "SearchAction",
  "target": {
    "@type": "EntryPoint",
    "urlTemplate": "https://mezzofy.com/search?q={search_term_string}"
  },
  "query-input": "required name=search_term_string"
}
```

---

### 2.6 Article datePublished Mismatch (Critical)

**Gap:** A factual inconsistency exists between visible HTML dates and JSON-LD dates:

| Article | HTML Visible Date | JSON-LD datePublished |
|---------|:-----------------:|:--------------------:|
| e-coupons-preference.html | **January 14, 2025** | ❌ 2026-02-19 |
| All other articles | Unknown (not visible) | 2026-02-19 (placeholder) |

AI engines that see the HTML date "January 14, 2025" and the JSON-LD date "2026-02-19" will flag this as conflicting signals, reducing content authority. **Fix immediately.**

---

### 2.7 NewsArticle Publisher Entity (Weak)

**Gap:** All 8 news articles have `"publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Mezzofy"}`. However, the news articles are about 3rd-party media coverage — Forbes China, CIO World, EJTech, etc. The `publisher` should reflect the original publisher, and Mezzofy should be the `about` entity.

**Current (incorrect for media coverage):**
```json
"publisher": {"name": "Mezzofy"}
```

**Recommended (correct for 3rd-party media coverage):**
```json
"publisher": {"@type": "NewsMediaOrganization", "name": "Forbes China", "url": "https://www.forbeschina.com"},
"about": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Mezzofy", "url": "https://mezzofy.com"},
"mentions": [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "Mezzofy"}]
```
*(Apply per article with appropriate publisher name)*

---

## Section 3: FAQ & Answer Engine Coverage (17/20)

### 3.1 FAQPage Coverage Map

| Page Category | Pages | FAQ in JSON-LD | FAQ visible in HTML | Q&A Count | Avg Answer Length |
|---------------|:-----:|:--------------:|:-------------------:|:---------:|:-----------------:|
| Solutions (4) | for-merchants/distributors/developers/ai-commerce | ✅ All 4 | ❌ None | 4 Q&As each | ~40-60 words |
| Products (7) | coupon-* | ✅ All 7 | ⚠️ coupon-nfc.html only | 3-4 Q&As each | ~40-60 words |
| Corporate (4) | investors, news-press, nfc-user-guide, support | ✅ nfc-user-guide only | ✅ coupon-nfc.html type | 4 Q&As | ~50 words |
| Core (3) | index, about, contact | ❌ None | ❌ None | 0 | — |
| Blog (6) | All | ❌ None | ❌ None | 0 | — |
| News (8) | All | ❌ None | ❌ None | 0 | — |

**Strength:** 18 pages (60%) have FAQ schema — above industry average.
**Gap:** Homepage (index.html) has no FAQ despite being the most likely page for AI to extract core company answers.

### 3.2 FAQ Answer Quality Assessment

| Criterion | Status | Notes |
|-----------|:------:|-------|
| Answers are self-contained (no "see above") | ✅ | All answers stand alone |
| Answers begin with a direct statement | ✅ | Most start with "Mezzofy...", "Yes.", "Zero." |
| Answer length 40-120 words | ✅ | Most within range |
| Technical specificity (HMAC, OAuth2, etc.) | ✅ | Good depth for technical queries |
| Natural language question phrasing | ✅ | "What is...", "How do...", "Does Mezzofy..." |
| Competitor comparison questions | ❌ | No "How does Mezzofy differ from X?" |
| Pricing/availability questions | ❌ | No FAQ about pricing or getting started |
| Integration questions | ⚠️ | Some pages cover POS/CRM, but incomplete |

### 3.3 Missing FAQ: Homepage

**The #1 AEO gap for AI citation readiness is having no FAQ on the homepage.**

AI engines asked general questions ("What is Mezzofy?", "What does Mezzofy do?", "What is the Global Coupon Exchange Protocol?") look for direct answers on the most authoritative page — the homepage.

**Recommended: Add FAQPage JSON-LD to index.html:**
```json
{
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What is Mezzofy?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Mezzofy is the Global Coupon Exchange Protocol — a tokenized B2B platform that connects merchants to distributors for secure digital coupon distribution. Founded in 2015 and headquartered in Hong Kong, Mezzofy has processed over 300 million digital coupons across Asia-Pacific."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What is the Global Coupon Exchange Protocol?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "The Global Coupon Exchange Protocol (GCEP) is Mezzofy's standardized infrastructure for digital coupon issuance, distribution, and redemption. It connects coupon supply (merchants) directly to demand (distributors and consumers) through cryptographically secured, tokenized coupon identifiers."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How many coupons has Mezzofy distributed?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Mezzofy has distributed over 300 million digital coupons across Asia-Pacific since its founding in 2015. The platform serves merchants, distributors, and consumers in Hong Kong and across the region."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What types of digital coupons does Mezzofy support?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Mezzofy supports QR code coupons, NFC tap-to-redeem coupons, serial number vouchers, digital wallet coupons (Apple Wallet, Google Pay, Samsung Wallet), and cash vouchers. All coupon types are managed through the Mezzofy Coupon Management System."
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## Section 4: Content Structure for Passage Indexing (11/15)

AI engines use passage-level indexing to extract specific answers from page content. Well-structured content with clear H2/H3 headings enables precise answer extraction.

### 4.1 Heading Hierarchy Assessment

| Page Type | H1 | H2 | H3 | Section Count | AI Indexability |
|-----------|----|----|----|:-------------:|:---------------:|
| Homepage | ✅ 1 | ✅ 4 | ✅ 15+ | Excellent | ✅ High |
| Solution pages | ✅ 1 | ✅ 4-5 | ✅ 8-12 | Excellent | ✅ High |
| Product pages | ✅ 1 | ✅ 4-6 | ✅ 8-14 | Excellent | ✅ High |
| Corporate pages | ✅ 1 | ✅ 3-5 | ✅ 5-10 | Good | ✅ High |
| Blog articles | ✅ 1 | ✅ 4-5 | ❌ 0 | Good | ✅ High |
| **News articles** | **✅ 1** | **❌ 0** | **❌ 0** | **Poor** | **⚠️ Low** |

**Critical issue: All 8 news articles lack H2 sub-sections.** They are single-block text under H1. AI passage indexing cannot extract specific facts from unstructured blocks.

### 4.2 News Article Structure Problem

**Current structure (all 8 news articles):**
```
H1: Article Title
[body paragraphs — 3-5 sentences]
[CTA section]
```

**Recommended structure:**
```
H1: Article Title
H2: [Publication/Coverage Context — e.g., "Forbes China Recognition"]
H2: About the [Award/Achievement]
H2: What This Means for Mezzofy
H2: About Mezzofy
```

Adding 2-3 H2 sections to each news article would dramatically improve AI passage extraction.

### 4.3 Statistical Data Points (Positive)

These specific claims across the site are high-value for AI citation:

| Statistic | Page | AEO Value |
|-----------|------|-----------|
| 300M+ digital coupons processed | index.html, ejtech-300m-coupons.html | ✅ High — unique, specific |
| 60% of consumers prefer e-coupons | e-coupons-preference.html | ✅ High — citable statistic |
| 77% of shoppers visit stores with discounts | e-coupons-preference.html | ✅ High |
| 240 PARKnSHOP locations | nfc-parknshop.html | ✅ High — specific case study |
| 900M+ AI users reachable | for-ai-commerce.html | ✅ High — notable claim |
| Founded 2015 | index.html JSON-LD | ✅ Medium |
| USD 2M Pre-Series A raised | funding-announcement.html | ✅ High — citable fact |
| 1-2 week NFC onboarding | coupon-nfc.html FAQ | ✅ Medium |

**These data points are excellent for AI citations and should be kept current.**

### 4.4 Date Metadata Discrepancy (Action Required)

| Issue | File | Severity |
|-------|------|:--------:|
| HTML shows "January 14, 2025" but JSON-LD has `2026-02-19` | e-coupons-preference.html | **Critical** |
| All other 13 articles show `2026-02-19` placeholder in JSON-LD | 13 article files | **High** |

AI engines check HTML visible dates against structured data. Mismatches reduce content authority.

---

## Section 5: E-E-A-T Signals for AI Credibility (5/10)

AI engines assess Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, and Trustworthiness before citing a source.

### 5.1 E-E-A-T Scorecard

| Signal | Present | Machine-Readable | Notes |
|--------|:-------:|:----------------:|-------|
| Company founding date (2015) | ✅ | ✅ JSON-LD foundingDate | Good |
| Industry awards (ESG, Environmental) | ✅ HTML | ❌ Not in schema | About page mentions awards but no `award` property in Organization schema |
| 3rd-party media coverage (Forbes, CIO World) | ✅ HTML | ⚠️ Partial (NewsArticle) | Good signal but publisher attribution is incorrect in schema |
| Founder identity (Dicky Yin) | ✅ HTML | ❌ No Person schema | Forbes article exists but no machine-readable Person entity |
| Social proof / LinkedIn | ✅ schema sameAs | ✅ | Only one social link |
| Contact information | ✅ contact.html | ❌ Not in schema contactPoint | AI can't extract email/phone |
| Specific case study (PARKnSHOP 240 stores) | ✅ HTML | ❌ Not in schema | Could add Case Study structured data |
| Funding evidence (USD 2M) | ✅ HTML+NewsArticle | ⚠️ In NewsArticle | Good but founder name missing |
| Security claims (HMAC, tokenized) | ✅ HTML + FAQ answers | ⚠️ In FAQ text | Good for technical queries |
| Location/headquarters | ❌ | ❌ | Neither HTML nor schema |

**Score: 5/10** — Multiple E-E-A-T signals exist in HTML but are not machine-readable in structured data.

### 5.2 Missing Author/Expert Markup on Blog Articles

Blog articles are attributed to "Mezzofy" (visible: line `<span>Mezzofy</span>`) with no specific author. For AI E-E-A-T evaluation:

| Current | Impact |
|---------|--------|
| `<span>Mezzofy</span>` as author text | Generic — no expertise demonstrated |
| Article JSON-LD: `"author": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Mezzofy"}` | Organization author is valid but weaker than Person author |
| No `<meta name="author">` tag | Minor — already handled in JSON-LD |

**Recommendation:** For blog articles, consider attributing to a named author (team member) and adding them as a Person schema. At minimum, add subject-matter expertise signals.

---

## Section 6: Voice & Conversational Readiness (4/10)

### 6.1 Speakable Schema (Missing)

`speakable` markup identifies content specifically optimized for voice assistants (Google Assistant, Siri, Alexa). None of the 30 pages include speakable markup.

**Recommended: Add to key pages (index.html, for-merchants.html, coupon-nfc.html):**
```json
"speakable": {
  "@type": "SpeakableSpecification",
  "cssSelector": [".hero-description", ".product-summary", "h1"]
}
```

### 6.2 Conversational Answer Quality

| Query Type | Coverage | Example |
|------------|:--------:|---------|
| "What is Mezzofy?" | ⚠️ | In meta desc + body; not as direct FAQ answer |
| "How does [product] work?" | ✅ | Covered by FAQPage on 18 pages |
| "What is [product name]?" | ✅ | FAQPage Q1 on every product page |
| "How much does Mezzofy cost?" | ❌ | No pricing FAQ anywhere |
| "Where is Mezzofy based?" | ❌ | Not in FAQ or schema |
| "Who founded Mezzofy?" | ❌ | Not in FAQ or Person schema |
| "How do I sign up for Mezzofy?" | ❌ | No onboarding FAQ |
| "What platforms does Mezzofy support?" | ⚠️ | Mentioned in some FAQs but not consolidated |
| "Does Mezzofy work with [major POS]?" | ⚠️ | Mentioned vaguely in playbook/management FAQs |

**7 of 10 common conversational queries are NOT directly answered in structured FAQs.**

---

### AEO Scoring per page (out of 100)

| Category | Weight | Criteria |
|----------|:------:|---------|
| Structured data completeness | 30 | Correct schemas + rich properties |
| FAQ/answer coverage | 25 | FAQPage present, answers direct & self-contained |
| Content passage structure | 20 | H1-H2-H3 hierarchy, section clarity |
| E-E-A-T signals | 15 | Authorship, credentials, verifiable claims |
| AI-specific signals | 10 | speakable, datePublished accuracy, entity links |

## Quick Wins (Can be done in 1 session)

These fixes require minimal code changes but deliver maximum AEO lift:

1. **robots.txt** — Add 3 bot rules (10 min)
2. **index.html** — Add FAQPage JSON-LD with 4 core Q&As (30 min)
3. **index.html + about.html** — Enrich Organization schema with founder, awards, contactPoint, location (30 min)
4. **e-coupons-preference.html** — Fix datePublished from `2026-02-19` to `2025-01-14` (5 min)
5. **llms.txt** — Expand page descriptions to 20-30 words each (30 min)
6. **about.html** — Add Person schema for Dicky Yin (15 min)

**Estimated total time: ~2 hours. Estimated AEO lift: +8 to +12 points site average.**
